## Windows shared-state service fix

This patch makes the Windows service honor the `serve`, `--addr`, and `--state` arguments persisted in its service ImagePath.

### Why it matters

- `csd` can now use the same SQLite coordination ledger as the interactive `cs` client.
- Durable messages, file touches, conflict warnings, and completion forwarding survive service restarts without splitting across user and LocalSystem state files.
- Explicit service configuration remains authoritative; no machine-wide `CODEX_SWARM_STATE` workaround is required.

### Verification

- Windows unit coverage proves persisted process arguments take precedence while preserving service-start argument fallback.
- Cross-platform test, vet, build, race, vulnerability, and workflow-lint gates.
- Live Windows service installation verifies v0.4.1 and the configured user-state path through `/status`.
